items: Create viewmodels if there are 2/3+ sets of information required
For edit endpoints, create a get required info function that can be used to refetch all the data that is required for displaying the form (do not overwrite form data with this function)
When an error is reached, refetch all the data with this

Also for edit VMs - use an object of the actual class itself to hold the edit data, do not replicate every field of the object being edited/created in the viewmodel, unless necessary for certain data types like DateTimes
otherwise just grab the info in the controller itself
Controller endpoints follow this general pattern:
Each endpoint should have a comment on it, just make it a simple sentence with a brief explanation of what this endpoint is for
add register routes function to Global.asax
converted UI controls to shared views
conceptually organize all files into areas/controllers
Going one controller at a time,
